answer:
**The detailed rules for ritual impurity (*āśauca* or *sūtaka*) following birth and death appear primarily in *Manusmṛti* chapter 5 (verses 57–116), with some continuation of purification topics into chapter 6.**[^1][^2]

Chapter 5 begins its discussion of these periods after addressing food prohibitions (e.g., avoiding food from a house "under impurity from childbirth" at [p-7851cc]).[^3] Bhrigu introduces the topic as follows:

> I will now declare the purification after a death, and likewise the purification of things, for all four *varṇas*, according to the rule and in due order.[^4]

Key rules include:

- All kinsmen (*sapiṇḍas*) observe impurity for deaths of children (with teeth, post-first-born, or post-tonsure), **and equivalently for births (*sūtaka*)**.[^5]
- Standard period for *sapiṇḍas* after death: **10 days**; reducible to 3 days (post-bone collection) or 1 day.[^6]
- Birth impurity mirrors death impurity for those seeking perfect purity; mother observes *sūtaka*, father purifies by water-touch (3 days recommended due to seed-connection).[^7]
- Graded by *varṇa* (for corpse-bearers or standard sapinda impurity): **Brāhmaṇa: 10 days; Kṣatriya: 12 days; Vaiśya: 15 days; Śūdra: 1 month**.[^8]
- Shorter for distant news (remainder of 10 days if <10 elapsed; 3 nights if >10 days; water-touch if >1 year).[^9]
- Miscarriage: nights equal to pregnancy months.[^10]
- Menstruation: bathing after flow ceases.[^11]

Full context requires reading verses 57–92 (death/birth) and 93–116 (special cases, objects). The wiki card flags chapter 5 signatures like *śāvam āśaucaṃ sapiṇḍeṣu* ("corpse-impurity for sapindas"), confirming focus here.[^12]

[^1]: *Manusmṛti*, chapter 5, [/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/05/translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/05/translation/).

[^2]: *Manusmṛti*, chapter 6 (continues purifications Mn 5.105ff.), [/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/).

[^3]: *Manusmṛti* 5 (= Mn_4.212[213M]), [p-7851cc](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/05/translation/#p-7851cc).

[^4]: *Manusmṛti* 5.57 (Mn_5.57), chapter 5.

[^5]: *Manusmṛti* 5.58 (Mn_5.58), [p-89d810](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/#p-89d810) (corpus places late ch.5 verses early in ch.6 body).

[^6]: *Manusmṛti* 5.59 (Mn_5.59).

[^7]: *Manusmṛti* 5.60–63 (Mn_5.60–63).

[^8]: *Manusmṛti* 5.83 (Mn_5.83).

[^9]: *Manusmṛti* 5.75–78 (Mn_5.75–78).

[^10]: *Manusmṛti* 5.66 (Mn_5.66).

[^11]: *Manusmṛti* 5.66 (Mn_5.66).

[^12]: Wiki card, *Manusmṛti* ch.5, ["āśaucaṃ sapiṇḍeṣu vidhīyate"](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/wiki/05.card.md).
